tiny_practice.action: Close your eyes and breathe deeply, imagining Jesus' resolve and surrender in the Garden of Gethsemane despite overwhelming emotional struggle

two_step.reflect_question: How do you respond when facing intense spiritual challenges or personal trials?

options.response: Practice intentional prayer and seek God's strength before difficult moments
Learn to submit your will to God's greater purpose, like Jesus did
Develop consistent spiritual disciplines to build resilience

creative_prompt.instruction: Draw Jesus praying in the garden, showing His emotions through the way you sketch His posture and facial expression

reevaluation.question: How has reading Luke 22 shifted your understanding of Jesus' humanity and divinity?
options: I see Jesus' emotional vulnerability more clearly
I understand the depth of His sacrificial love
I recognize His strength in facing betrayal
I'm more aware of His commitment to God's plan

three_choice.question: Which moment reveals the deepest human struggle in this chapter?

choices.expansion: The complexity of human weakness and moral compromise emerges through a calculated act of treachery.
The fragility of human courage confronts us when personal safety conflicts with loyalty.
The profound internal conflict between divine mission and human vulnerability becomes starkly visible.

socratic_nudge.question: If divine foreknowledge exists, does human agency truly matter in moments of critical moral choice?

moral_slider.question: How do personal loyalty and spiritual commitment balance in moments of extreme pressure?

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of sacrifice and commitment shifted?
options: Recognized the complexity of human weakness
Saw how personal fear can override moral conviction
Appreciated the depth of Jesus's compassion

thematic_summaries.title: The Betrayal and Covenant
thematic_summaries.summary: Luke 22 reveals the profound tension between divine sovereignty and human betrayal. Judas' betrayal of Jesus represents a critical moment of spiritual conflict, demonstrating how human weakness intersects with God's redemptive plan.

thematic_summaries.title: The New Covenant in Christ's Blood
thematic_summaries.summary: Jesus establishes the New Covenant through the Last Supper, symbolically connecting His sacrificial death with the forgiveness of sins. This moment represents the theological transition from Old Testament sacrificial system to a new relationship with God through Christ's atonement.

reevaluation.question: How does Jesus' anticipation of suffering in Gethsemane reveal the complexity of divine will and human experience?
options: Jesus demonstrates complete submission to the Father's plan
Human emotional vulnerability coexists with divine purpose
Spiritual warfare is manifested through Christ's internal struggle
Divine foreknowledge does not eliminate genuine human experience of pain
